name: Homemade Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: Blend until mixed well, serve cold.
Stores up to two weeks in the refrigerator, but it's never lasted that long.

name: Bailey's Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: Combine all ingredients in a blender for 1 minute.
Let stand overnight.
Bottle the next day.

name: Irish Cream (Almost Like Bailey's)
directions: Mix in blender or with wire whip.
Refrigerate or freeze. Serve over  crushed ice.

name: Irish Cream
directions: Put all ingredients in blender until smooth.
Put in a bottle; a liter is best because is almost fills it.
Place in a refrigerator for 24 hours to rest.
Keep refrigerated.
Enjoy.

name: Bailey's Irish Cream II
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender.
Store in the refrigerator.

name: Eagle Brand Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: Mix all ingredients together in a blender until smooth.
Keep refrigerated and shake before pouring.
Store refrigerated for up to one month.
Makes about 4 cups.
*Use only clean, uncracked eggs.

name: Homemade Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: In a mixer beat the eggs,beat in the remaining ingredients until smooth and well blended.
Pour into a bottle and cork, store in the refrigerator (this can be stored for up to a month).
Serve over Ice.

name: Irish Cream (A substitute for "Bailey's)
directions: Put all ingredients into a blender until well blended.
Refrigerate for several hours.

name: Irish Cream
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender.
Store in a dark bottle in the refrigerator.

name: Homemade Irish Cream Liqueur (With Eggs)
directions: In a 6 cup blender, at low speed, combine all ingredients.
Blend until smooth.
Pour into clean bottles, cap tightly and store in the refrigerator.
It will keep up to one month.
Stir or shake well before serving.
Can be served straight, on the rocks, over ice cream or used in recipes calling for Irish Cream Liqueur.
Recipe makes 44.75oz = 1323.42ml.
A serving size is 1.3oz (37ml)  (37.0g) = 35 servings per recipe.

name: Irish Cream Liqueur (Eggless Version)
directions: Place first 3 ingredients in a blender and process at low speed for 10 seconds.
Add vanilla and almond extracts and condensed milk, blend again for 1 minute.
Add rye whiskey and blend again for 25 seconds.
Can be served straight, on the rocks, in mixed drinks or over ice cream.
Store tightly capped in the refrigerator for up to one month.
Recipe makes 38oz = 1123.79ml (approx).
A serving size is 1.3oz (37ml) = 29.23 servings per recipe.

name: Homemade Irish Cream
directions: Blend all ingredients in blender for 1 minute on medium setting.
Refrigerate.
Will keep for 1 month in refrigerator.
Use like Bailey's Irish Cream.

name: O'Brian's Irish Cream
directions: Mix all ingredients in blender.
Pour into pretty bottles and refrigerate.
Keeps up to 3 months.

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Place 1 tbsp brown sugar in bottom of each clear goblet.
Add jigger of Irish Mist to each glass and stir.
Place teaspoon in bottom of glass and slowly pour coffee into goblet until about 1/4" from top of glass.
(The spoon wil protect goblet from cracking from heat of coffee) Move teaspoon to edge of coffee on top of glass.
SLOWLY pour heavy cream into spoon allowing overfill into glass.
If you do it correctly, the heavy cream will float on top of coffee.
DO NOT STIR.
SIP COFFEE THRU THE CREAM.
Delightful.

name: Fabulous Faux Bailey's Irish Cream
directions: Blend all ingredients together and chill.
Serve over ice, or in coffee.
Refrigerate leftovers (in the unlikely event that there will be any--).

name: Irish Baileys
directions: Dissolve the sugar with the hot water.
Leave to cool.
Add the whiskey then leave until cold.
Mix the evaporated milk with the cocoa.
add to the cold mixture.
Ready to drink immediately.

name: Baileys Berry Frappe
directions: Blend all ingredients with 2-3 ice cubes. Serve in a glass over crushed ice. Garnish with a strawberry.

name: Sheila's Irish Cream Liqueur
directions: Combine all ingredients in blender.
Blend on high until well mixed and foamy.
Serve (or freeze for later use).

name: Irish Coffee Teddy's Mommy Style
directions: Brew coffee.
Mix in shots of Whisky and Irish Cream.
Top with Whipped Cream.

name: Baileys Coffee Frappe
directions: Blend two heaped teaspoons of instant coffee with one small cup of boiling water.  Blend all ingredients with 2-3 ice cubes. Garnish with coffee beans.

name: Maloney's Irish Cream
directions: Stir sugar and 2 tablespoons water in heavy saucepan over medium heat until sugar dissolves and syrup comes to simmer. Cool.

Combine 2 tablespoons raw-sugar syrup, 1/4 cup whiskey, 2 tablespoons cream, 1/4 teaspoon Scotch, and 1/8 teaspoon (generous) orange-flower water in cocktail shaker; add 1 cup ice cubes and cover tightly. Shake drink vigorously at least 30 seconds to chill and aerate. Pour drink with ice cubes in tumbler. Repeat with remaining ingredients to made second drink.

name: Cappuccino With Irish Cream
directions: Using an espresso machine or cappuccino maker, brew espresso.
Steam milk and Bailys until hot and frothy.
Put 1 teaspoon sugar into 2 serving mugs.
Divide espresso and steamed milk into serving mugs.
Stir and enjoy.

name: Chocolate Irish Coffee
directions: Place dark chocolate in Irish coffee mug and microwave until melted, about 1 to 1 1/2 minutes.  Add hot brewed coffee and stir well, until chocolate and coffee are well blended. Add whiskey.
Top with a dollop of whipped cream, then drizzle creme de menthe on top of the whipped cream mound.  If desired, garnish with chocolate shavings or cocoa powder.
Serve hot.

name: Irish Chocolate
directions: Heat and mix the ingredients.
Froth and serve the chocolate in 4 individual cups. Enjoy!

name: Italian Hot Chocolate
directions: Heat and mix all ingredients.
Froth for 2 minutes.
Dispense into cups and top with whipped cream.

name: Leprechaun's Latte
directions: Brew 175-235 ml (6-8 oz.) of Van Houtte Holiday Blend coffee directly into a 415 ml (14 oz.) mug.
While the coffee is brewing, warm the milk using a milk frother.
When coffee is ready, add 120 ml (4 oz.) of warm milk to coffee.
Add the Irish cream liqueur.
Top with whipped cream.
